title: Verilog names in JSON output
body:
Hi!

I use JSON output of a netlist to generate some new Verilog code and have a question about the original Verilog names of the netlist.

There are "name" and "origName" fields in produced JSON dump, but as I see, they are not exactly Verilog source identifiers, for example if it is an escaped identifier, these fields omit leading backslash and trailing whitespace and "origName" replaces escaped symbols with other sequences of symbols, e.g. `"name":"escaped?cellname[0]"` with `"origName":"escaped__03fcellname__05b0__05d"`
At first sight, the fastest solution to find out whether the name should be escaped is to just compare lengths of "name" and "origName" (if "origName" is larger that means there are escaped symbols) but I discovered some cases for which this solution does not work:
- if identifier contains double underscores then second underscore is also replaced and "origName" is larger but the identifier shouldn't be escaped (e.g. `"name":"multi__underscore___cellname"` with `"multi___05Funderscore___05F_cellname"`).
- if there are parameters for a module then "name" is append with some id (e.g. `"name":"PARAMETRIZED_MOD__Iz1"` for ` module PARAMETRIZED_MOD #(parameter [3:0] INIT = 4'b0000)`)

I can somehow handle all this stuff of course but I may not know about some other pitfalls so I wonder if there is some way to get the original Verilog names in produced JSON maybe, or a reliable way of obtaining those.
